title: Pretraining as the Base
concept: Pretraining builds the base model (a text predictor); instruction and alignment stages turn it into an assistant. Boss explain ends with: 'Next up: Model Scale: Parameters & Layers.'

exercises.prompt: Why is pretraining considered the 'base' stage of an AI?

options.text: It builds the base knowledge by reading massive text.
It teaches the model to follow safety rules and guidelines.
It makes the model run faster on mobile phones.

exercises.explain: Pretraining acts as the foundation, teaching the model language patterns before it learns to be a helpful assistant.

cards.title: Building the AI Foundation
cards.body: Ever wonder how ChatGPT got so smart? It didn't start out as a helpful assistant. It began its life as a giant autocomplete machine, reading the internet to learn how to predict the next word!

cards.title: What is Pretraining?
cards.body: Pretraining is the first phase of training an AI model. By reading massive amounts of text from the internet, the model learns grammar, facts, and how to predict the very next word in a sentence. This creates a 'base model.'

cards.title: The Language Sponge
cards.body: Think of pretraining like teaching a toddler every word in the dictionary and reading them millions of stories. They learn how language works, but they don't know how to act like a polite, helpful assistant yet. They are just a giant word sponge!

cards.title: The Base Model in Action
cards.body: If you ask a pretrained base model: 'Can you help me write a poem?', it might not write a poem. Instead, it might just predict the next logical words: '...or can you write a story instead?' because it is just auto-completing text, not acting like an assistant!

cards.title: Myth: Pretrained Means Ready
cards.body: Many people think an AI is ready to help you right after it finishes its first round of training. In reality, a raw pretrained model is chaotic and unpredictable. It requires extra 'fine-tuning' and 'alignment' to learn how to listen to your instructions.

cards.title: Base Model vs. Assistant
cards.body: See the difference between the raw starting point and the polished final helper.

right.text: Trained with feedback to answer questions, follow safety rules, and behave like a helpful guide.

left.text: Reads the whole web to predict the next word. It autocompletes text but doesn't follow directions.

exercises.prompt: Why can't we easily use a raw pretrained 'base model' as a chatbot assistant?

options.text: It does not know any human languages yet.
It only knows how to auto-complete text, not how to follow instructions.
It is too small to answer any questions.
It can only understand computer code, not English.

exercises.explain: Pretraining builds a base model that predicts text, while instruction and alignment train it to act like a helpful assistant.

exercises.prompt: Pretraining is the first stage of building an AI assistant.
exercises.explain: True! Pretraining is the very first stage where the model learns from vast amounts of text. Instruction tuning comes later.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the description of a base model's core task.
exercises.sentence: A pretrained base model is designed to _______ the next word.
exercises.explain: A base model is trained to predict the most likely next word based on all the text it has read.

exercises.prompt: Which response is most likely from a raw pretrained base model (just predicting next words) when asked: 'Write a story about a dragon:'?

options.text: AI 1: 'Can you help me write a story about a dragon?'
AI 2: 'Once upon a time, in a land far away, a fiery dragon...'
AI 3: 'Sure! Here is a story about a brave dragon.'

exercises.explain: The AI assistant responds directly to the prompt, whereas the base model simply autocompletes or mimics the prompt format.

exercises.prompt: What is the main goal of the pretraining stage for an AI model?

options.text: To make it immediately ready to act as a helpful chatbot
To teach it the basic structure of language and general facts from text
To teach it to strictly follow safety rules and avoid bad words
To run it on small mobile devices without internet connection

exercises.explain: Pretraining creates the raw brain (the foundation), while instruction and alignment teach it how to behave. Next up: Model Scale: Parameters & Layers.
